experiment: lane ZR THE FINISH WAVE: seagull.glb rig feasibility test (doc 30 sec RIG CANDIDATES INVENTORY)

models.name: seagull

attempts.error: Pose estimation failed, please provide a valid model URL
Pose estimation failed, please provide a valid model URL

planned_anim_if_rig_succeeded.reason_picked: closest cyclic, symmetric two-limb up/down motion in the public catalog (680 entries, all humanoid rigType) to a wing flap -- confirmed via full-catalog keyword search (fly/flap/wing/bird/glide/soar/hover/flutter all came back empty or off-topic; e.g. 'Flying Fist Kick' is a martial kick, not a flap). Never reached: rig itself never produced a rig_task_id to animate against.

models.verdict: RIG FAILED (2/2 attempts, both HTTP 422 'Pose estimation failed'). Meshy's rigging docs state the endpoint supports only 'standard humanoid (bipedal) assets with clearly defined limbs' and explicitly rejects non-humanoid assets; the seagull's quadruped-ish/avian body plan (two legs + two folded wings, no arms) never produces the pose estimate the auto-rig needs, at either the in-game display height (0.6m) or the API's own human-default height (1.7m) -- confirms this is a body-plan rejection, not a scale/parameter issue. No task id was ever created (pre-flight validation, not a generation failure), so 0 credits were consumed on either attempt. KEEP the existing empty-wing-pivot version (minigames/tilt/seagull.gd TiltSeagull, core/ambient_life.gd Seagull) -- no code changes made. This closes the doc-30 feasibility caveat: auto-rig genuinely cannot rig this asset; the documented fallback (hand-split wing mesh, zero further Meshy spend) is the only remaining path if a real flap is wanted, and is out of scope for this API-only wave.
